channel.title: Design pattern on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Design pattern on Frank Huang

item.title: Design Patterns that are useful part 2 -- implemented in python
item.description: Again, these patterns are not supposed to cover all of the pattern that exists in the tech world. Patterns like Singleton, Factory, Builder .etc might not show up here since they are quite common in most code bases. I would only note those that I think might be helpful for my or your future career. Prototype pattern A creation pattern which allow faster creation of object with a simple clone method.

item.title: Design Patterns that are useful part 1 -- implemented in python
item.description: Bridge pattern For bridge pattern, we define 2 classes that in charge of different things. First one I would call it orchestrator but essentially is the so called bridge which in charge of calling corresponding functions. This class is also exposed to the external client. Second one I would call it instrument which should be an interface or abstract class defining what the . The instance of this class will be aggregated into the orchestrator so that the orchestrator can call whatever functions.

item.title: Strategy pattern
item.description: The Strategy Pattern defines a family of algorithms, encapsulates each one, and makes them interchangeable. Strategy lets the algorithm vary independently from clients that use it. This is quoted from the book called . From my daily development experience, I encountered a lot of class end with impl or start with I. I know they are meant for implementation and interface. But how come they are becoming some kind of must have for every java project that I encountered.

item.title: Dynamic proxy in Java
item.description: Proxy your call to the professional, save you some more time I recently read a great slides talking about Proxy pattern in Java which has confused me for a long time. I’ve always consider it as something like the delegator pattern which is like adding a new layer for a specified class so that clients can do more other things when calling it. It turns out that there could be more to do with java dynamic proxy.
